clusters.name: Dark Pattern Taxonomy & Classification
clusters.summary: Three complementary taxonomies: Brignull's 16-type practitioner taxonomy, a 68-type academic taxonomy (6 categories, 32 subcategories), and CHI 2024's synthesized 64-type ontology across 3 levels. Instagram evolution study adds 4 new types. Confirmshaming, roach motel (hard-to-cancel), hidden costs, forced continuity, misdirection, privacy zuckering, bait-and-switch, and disguised ads are core Brignull patterns.

clusters.name: Automated Dark Pattern Detection
clusters.summary: Detection approaches: CV (UIGuard F1=0.79, YOLOv12x real-time), NLP (BERT, DistilBERT for text), DOM analysis, and multi-modal pipelines. Current tools cover only 45.5% of pattern types. LLM agents themselves are vulnerable to dark patterns (41% susceptibility, rising to 80% with multiple patterns). Open-source tools: AidUI, Dapde, CogniGuard, DarkPatternLLM.

clusters.name: Regulatory Landscape (EU, US, Global)
clusters.summary: EU DSA Article 25 first explicit dark pattern prohibition (Feb 2024). Digital Fairness Act Q4 2026 for coherence. FTC: $2.5B Amazon settlement, Click-to-Cancel vacated but enforcement continues under ROSCA/Section 5. CPRA: $2,500-$7,500/violation, symmetry requirement. ICPEN: 76% of subscription sites use dark patterns. Insurance sector (IRDAI) expanding scope.

clusters.name: Cookie Consent UX & Compliance
clusters.summary: EDPB requires equal prominence accept/reject, no pre-ticked boxes, prior blocking, granular consent. 93% of users never go past first screen. EU admits system is 'long overdue' for fix. DNT failed. Article 88b proposes machine-readable signals but may repeat failure. Open-source CMPs: Klaro, Osano (2B+ monthly), consent.io. Cookie pop-ups waste 575M hours/year.

clusters.name: Subscription UX: Sign-up/Cancel Parity
clusters.summary: Core problem: asymmetrical effort (easy join, hard leave). FTC Click-to-Cancel vacated but precedent set. Amazon $2.5B settlement. Loss Aversion Manipulation, Emotional Exploitation, Hidden Options most common cancellation dark patterns. 76% of sites use patterns. Cancellation must be as easy as signup -- same channel, same effort.

clusters.name: Manipulative Urgency vs Legitimate Scarcity
clusters.summary: 40% of countdown timers are fake. Fake scarcity increases frustration/stress/irritation. Legitimate urgency: real deadlines, backend-connected inventory, verifiable claims. Combining real inventory + real time pressure is effective and honest. Fake urgency erodes trust despite short-term conversion lift.

clusters.name: Deceptive UI Hierarchy & Pre-checked Options
clusters.summary: Visual prominence manipulation: large/bold preferred action, small/pale alternatives. Pre-checked checkboxes exploit default effect. Double negatives in opt-outs. High-contrast accept vs low-contrast reject. Princeton/UChicago: 10%+ of 11K e-commerce sites use deceptive patterns. Privacy settings that reset after updates are a systematic dark pattern.

clusters.name: Trust Architecture & Ethical Alternatives
clusters.summary: Ethical design framework: transparency, consistency, error recovery. CHI 2024 concept-based framework shifts from pattern-avoidance to proactive ethics. Ethical gamification: visible progress, user autonomy, no forced participation. Honest UX converts better long-term. Quarterly ethics audits recommended. Remove false urgency, make accessibility standard.

clusters.name: Notification Permission Patterns
clusters.summary: Pre-permission (double-permission) pattern: custom dialog first, native prompt only if user agrees. Never prompt on page load. Best timing: after relevant user action. Communicate value clearly. Cost-benefit analysis drives user decisions. Pre-permission preserves native prompt for when user is ready.

clusters.name: Account Deletion & Right to Erasure
clusters.summary: EDPB 2025 enforcement focus on Article 17 GDPR right to erasure. Most frequently exercised and complained-about right. Key gaps: no systematic data classification, no automated deletion labels, procedural inconsistencies. Must respond within 1 month. CNIL, CNPD, IMY using findings for 2026 inspections. Need: data mapping, erasure classes, clear deletion protocols.

clusters.name: Industry Awareness & Implementation Gap
clusters.summary: 61% of practitioners aware their apps contain dark patterns, only 30% have mitigation measures. A/B testing enables hiding unethical design. Dark patterns expanding to insurance (IRDAI), financial services. The implementation-practice disconnect is the core systemic problem.

gaps: Need more empirical data on dark pattern detection accuracy across different industries
Limited coverage of mixed-reality/VR dark patterns (referenced in ACM but not deeply explored)
Accessibility intersection with dark patterns (how patterns disproportionately affect disabled users)

agent_generation_assessment.reason: 11 clusters with 5+ actionable items each across taxonomy, detection, regulation, and ethical alternatives. Sufficient depth for a dark-pattern-auditor agent or ethical-design-reviewer skill.
suggested_capabilities: UI screenshot analysis for dark patterns
